mission.title: Rotate kubeadm control-plane certificates and refresh kubeconfig
mission.description: Production-grade runbook for kubeadm-managed clusters when certificates are near expiry or the console preflight reports EXPIRED_CREDENTIALS. The workflow backs up PKI assets, renews control-plane certificates, refreshes kubeconfig credentials, restarts kubelet, and verifies the API server is healthy again.

steps.title: Verify cluster access and kubeadm availability
steps.description: Confirm the current host can reach the cluster API server and that kubeadm is present before touching certificate material.
steps.failureHandling: If kubectl cannot reach the API server, stop and restore connectivity first. If kubeadm is missing, run this procedure from a kubeadm control-plane node or install the matching kubeadm package.

steps.title: Inspect certificate expiration windows
steps.description: Review kubeadm-managed certificate expiration so you can confirm the rotation target and capture the pre-change state.
commands:
sudo kubeadm certs check-expiration
sudo openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -noout -enddate
steps.validation: sudo test -f /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt && sudo kubeadm certs check-expiration > /dev/null 2>&1
steps.failureHandling: If /etc/kubernetes/pki is missing, this is not the right control-plane host. If kubeadm reports externally managed certificates, switch to your external PKI renewal process instead of kubeadm certificate renewal.

steps.title: Back up PKI and admin kubeconfig files
steps.description: Create a timestamped backup of the Kubernetes PKI directory and admin kubeconfig before renewing anything.
commands:
BACKUP_DIR="/var/backups/kubeadm-pki-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
sudo mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"
sudo cp -a /etc/kubernetes/pki "$BACKUP_DIR"/
sudo cp /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf "$BACKUP_DIR"/admin.conf
echo "$BACKUP_DIR"
steps.failureHandling: If the backup step fails, do not continue. Confirm the filesystem has free space and that sudo works non-interactively for the operator running the mission.

steps.title: Renew kubeadm-managed certificates
steps.description: Renew all kubeadm-managed control-plane certificates and regenerate the standard kubeconfig files that embed client certificates.
commands:
sudo kubeadm certs renew all
sudo kubeadm init phase kubeconfig admin
sudo kubeadm init phase kubeconfig controller-manager
sudo kubeadm init phase kubeconfig scheduler
steps.failureHandling: If renewal fails, restore /etc/kubernetes/pki and /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf from the backup created in step 3, then investigate kubeadm output before trying again.

steps.title: Restart kubelet so static pods reload certificates
steps.description: Restart kubelet on the control-plane node to force static pod manifests to re-read the renewed certificates and kubeconfigs.
commands:
sudo systemctl restart kubelet
sudo systemctl is-active kubelet
steps.failureHandling: If kubelet does not return to active state, inspect journalctl for kubelet startup errors and restore the PKI backup if the renewed files are malformed.

steps.title: Refresh the operator kubeconfig and verify cluster health
steps.description: Copy the regenerated admin kubeconfig into the operator home directory, then verify the API server and nodes are healthy with the renewed credentials.
commands:
mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube"
sudo cp /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf "$HOME/.kube/config"
sudo chown "$USER" "$HOME/.kube/config"
chmod 600 "$HOME/.kube/config"
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready node --all --timeout=300s
steps.failureHandling: If kubectl still reports expired credentials, confirm the refreshed file is the one referenced by KUBECONFIG. On multi-control-plane clusters, repeat certificate sync and kubelet restart on the remaining control-plane nodes.

resolution.summary: Control-plane certificates and kubeconfig client certificates have been rotated successfully. This runbook specifically addresses EXPIRED_CREDENTIALS preflight failures on kubeadm-managed clusters.

troubleshooting.title: Cluster uses externally managed certificates
troubleshooting.description: If kubeadm reports externally managed certificates, stop using this runbook and rotate the certificates through your enterprise PKI pipeline instead.

troubleshooting.title: Only one control-plane node was renewed
troubleshooting.description: On HA clusters, renew and distribute certificates on each control-plane node. A single stale apiserver certificate can keep the load balancer serving an expired endpoint.

prerequisites.resources: Access to a kubeadm control-plane node with sudo privileges.
prerequisites.description: Use on kubeadm-managed clusters only. The operator must have shell access to a control-plane node and permission to restart kubelet.
